====On hand==== {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |main=Leon's Charizard |stage1=Charmander |stage2=Charmeleon |pkmn=Charizard |giga=yes |type1=Fire |type2=Flying |img=Leon Charizard.png |cap=Leon's Charizard |img2=Leon Gigantamax Charizard.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Charizard |epnum=JN012 |epname=Flash of the Titans! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Kenta Miyake |desc={{p|Charizard}} is Leon's [[List of characters' first Pokémon|first Pokémon]], as well as his ace and capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. It was first seen being used in Leon's battle against {{an|Lance}} in the finals of the [[World Coronation Series]] and won. In ''[[JN131|Paring Pokémon While Parrying!]]'', Charizard was Leon's sixth Pokémon during his Masters Eight Tournament match against Ash. After Gigantamaxing, Charizard clashed with Ash's Pikachu's {{m|10,000,000 Volt Thunderbolt}} [[Z-Move]], which caused {{TP|Leon|Eternatus}} to awaken and appear at [[Wyndon Stadium]]. After being briefly recalled in favor of {{p|Cinderace}} in [[JN132|the next episode]], Charizard resumed its battle with Pikachu. Both Pokémon gave it their all, with Charizard ultimately falling and suffering its first loss in an official battle, thus causing Leon to lose the match.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Dragapult |type1=Dragon |type2=Ghost |img=Leon Dragapult.png |epnum=JN043 |epname=Sword and Shield: The Darkest Day! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=List of English voice actors{{!}}Daman Mills |vaennote=Dreepy |vaen2=Marc Thompson |vaen2note=Dragapult |desc={{p|Dragapult}} is Leon's second known Pokémon. In its debut appearance, Leon used it to battle a {{p|Coalossal}} that had [[Gigantamax]]ed alongside [[Ash's Pikachu]] and {{AP|Gengar}}, eventually taking it down. In ''[[JN100|Meeting Up with the Monarch!]]'', Dragapult was used in Leon's training. Sometime later, Leon used Dragapult to confront a pair of {{p|Corviknight}} that attacked him and {{Ash}}. In ''[[JN122|It's... Champion Time!]]'', Dragapult was one of the three Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against [[Diantha]]. It was shown battling her {{p|Hawlucha}} and {{p|Tyrantrum}} before being defeated by the latter. In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Leon used Dragapult as his fourth Pokémon in his Masters Eight Tournament battle against Ash, where it fought against {{AP|Dracovish}}, {{AP|Lucario}}, and {{AP|Dragonite}}. It was able to defeat [[Mega Evolution|Mega]] Lucario, before losing to Dragonite. Dragapult's known moves are {{m|Dragon Darts}}, {{m|Dragon Rush}}, {{m|Flamethrower}}, {{m|Thunderbolt}}, and {{m|Dragon Tail}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Rillaboom |giga=yes |type1=Grass |img=Leon Rillaboom.png |cap=Leon's Rillaboom |img2=Leon Gigantamax Rillaboom.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Rillaboom |epnum=JN115 |epname=Curtain Up! Fight the Fights! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Bill Rogers |desc={{p|Rillaboom}} is Leon's third known Pokémon. Like Charizard, it is capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing. It was the first Pokémon that Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] battle against [[Alain]]. It went up against his {{p|Chesnaught}} and won, only to lose to [[Alain's Charizard]] afterwards. In ''[[JN122|It's... Champion Time!]]'', Rillaboom was one of the three Pokémon Leon used in his Masters Eight Tournament match against [[Diantha]]. It was shown defeating her {{p|Tyrantrum}}, {{p|Aurorus}}, {{p|Gourgeist}}, and {{p|Goodra}}, before losing to {{TP|Diantha|Gardevoir}}. In ''[[JN130|Toying With Your Motions!]]'', Rillaboom was sent out to battle [[Ash's Dragonite]] as Leon's fifth Pokémon in the Masters Eight Tournament finals. In [[JN131|the next episode]], it battled and defeated Dragonite and {{AP|Sirfetch'd}}, before losing to {{AP|Dracovish}}. Rillaboom's known moves are {{m|Acrobatics}}, {{m|Drum Beating}}, {{m|High Horsepower}}, and {{m|Knock Off}}.
As a Gigantamax Rillaboom, it can use {{m|G-Max Drum Solo}}, {{m|Max Airstream}}, and {{m|Max Quake}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Cinderace |giga=yes |type1=Fire |img=Leon Cinderace.png |cap=Leon's Cinderace |img2=Leon Gigantamax Cinderace.png |cap2=Leon's Gigantamax Cinderace |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen= |desc={{p|Cinderace}} is Leon's fourth known Pokémon. It was the first Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Pikachu}}. Using its {{a|Libero}} [[Ability]] to [[Type change|become]] a {{t|Ground}} type, Cinderace was able to make Ash [[recall]] Pikachu before being recalled itself. In ''[[JN131|Paring Pokémon While Parrying!]]'', Cinderace was sent out to battle {{AP|Dracovish}}, defeating it before being recalled for Charizard. In [[JN132|the next episode]], it was revealed to be capable of [[Gigantamax]]ing as it fought against Gigantamax Pikachu, only to be defeated. Cinderace's known moves are {{m|Scorching Sands}}, {{m|High Jump Kick}}, {{m|Pyro Ball}}, and {{m|Iron Head}}, and its [[Ability]] is {{a|Libero}}.
As a Gigantamax Cinderace, it can use {{m|G-Max Fireball}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Inteleon |gender=male |type1=Water |img=Leon Inteleon.png |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Barrett Leddy |desc={{p|Inteleon}} is Leon's fifth known Pokémon. He was the second Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where he went up against {{AP|Gengar}}. Despite forming a [[Counter Shield]] against Gengar, Inteleon was soon defeated by Gengar after it [[Gigantamax]]ed. Inteleon's known moves are {{m|Snipe Shot}}, {{m|Dark Pulse}}, and {{m|Aqua Jet}}.}} {{TrainerPoké |trainer=Leon |pkmn=Mr. Rime |type1=Ice |type2=Psychic |img=Leon Mr Rime.png |epnum=JN129 |epname=A Flood of Torrential Gains! |vajp=Kenta Miyake |vaen=Billy Bob Thompson |desc={{p|Mr. Rime}} is Leon's sixth known Pokémon. It was the third Pokémon Leon used in his [[Masters Eight Tournament]] match against {{Ash}}, where it went up against {{AP|Gengar}}. Despite being struck by {{a|Cursed Body}}, Mr. Rime was able to defeat Gengar. In [[JN130|the next episode]], it fought {{AP|Sirfetch'd}} and [[Mega Evolution|Mega]] {{AP|Lucario}}, ultimately losing to the latter. Mr. Rime's known moves are {{m|Freeze-Dry}}, {{m|Psychic Terrain}}, {{m|Expanding Force}}, and {{m|Triple Axel}}.}}
